title: amellus tridactylus subsp. olivaceus
intro: Amellus tridactylus subsp. olivaceus, also known as the thorny cushion or cushion spurge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Amellus tridactylus subsp. olivaceus include its potential to treat various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or oil, and can be used topically or internally, but further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
